title: papaver alpinum subsp. sendtneri
intro: Papaver alpinum subsp. sendtneri, also known as the Alpine Poppy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the poppy family. It is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, characterized by its delicate white flowers with a hint of blue. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its alkaloids, primarily including papaverine and thebaine. Traditionally, the Alpine Poppy has been used to treat various health issues, including pain relief, fever reduction, and respiratory problems, with herbal preparations often involving infusions, teas, or tinctures of the plant's dried flowers and leaves.
